titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.37

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=628 status=live modified=2022-11-01 13:09:42.834437+00 | translation moa=629 status=live modified=2022-11-01 13:09:41.930636+00

p.cl:
. W. Permissione Diuina Ecclesie Sancti Andree Minister Humilis Dilectis Suis . R . Decano de Liston’. et Magistro . R .
De Doure Rectori ecclesie de Tinigham . et domino . I . de Penthcadland’ . Salutem .

Mandamus uobis Quatinus in uigilia
sancti Mathei apostoli apud Hadingtona in propriis personis accedatis ad taxandum uicariam de Hadingtona auctoritate nostra ad
ualenciam . XL . marcarum . Et si Senescallus noster dictis die et Loco ad hoc faciendum una uobiscum interesse non potuerit  ad
gerendum uices dicti senescalli nostri  mittemus ad uos prenominato die domini . R . de Methfen capellanum nostrum . Hoc autem
mandatum nostrum nullo modo facere omitteritis .

William by divine permission humble servant of the church of St Andrews to his well beloved Ralph, dean of Liston, and Master Richard of Dover, rector of the church of Tyninghame, and the lord John of Pencaitland, greeting.

We command you that on the vigil of Saint Matthew the apostle, at Haddington, you undertake an assessment of the vicarage of Haddington by our authority to the value of forty marks. And if our steward cannot be present on the said day and place to do this with you, I shall send you on the aforenamed day the lord Robert of Methven, our chaplain, to act as the said steward’s deputy. By no means fail to carry out this our mandate.
